description: A strong magnetic metal used for tools and construction; the metal of strength and industry. 'Tesu' names what endures—the backbone of building and making.
sound_symbolism: The word strikes strong. 'Te' lands with firm impact, hammer on anvil. Then 'su' sustains with enduring strength, iron holding.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo tesu' (the iron). Stronger than 'roena' (copper). For tools, building, the industrial metal.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Iron determination—the quality of unwavering commitment, strong as forged metal.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Iron changed everything—tools, weapons, plows, the metal that built nations.
